notes: On https://claude.ai/new, the authenticated Claude page loaded and a visible accessible button named 'Open PromptMate' was present.
Unsupported-site sentinel checked on https://www.google.com/. Exact visible PromptMate text count was 0 and no PromptMate control appeared in the DOM snapshot.
Clicking 'Open PromptMate' opened the complementary panel. Snapshot confirmed Search prompts, Tone & Format, grouped prompt library, PromptMate menu, and New prompt controls.
Clicking the panel header Close button removed the panel and restored exactly one 'Open PromptMate' button.
After reopening the panel, clicking the floating button named 'Close PromptMate' did not close it. A fresh DOM snapshot still showed the complementary panel and the Close PromptMate button.
Searching for existing title 'deepseek1' produced a single result header and showed only the matching prompt card.
Searching for body-only token 'ds1' returned the prompt titled 'deepseek1', whose visible body is 'ds1 2 latest'.
Query 'xyznotfound' displayed '0 results for "xyznotfound"' and 'No prompts match "xyznotfound".'
Clicking the uniquely identified Clear search button removed the filtered empty state and restored the grouped prompt library.
Both lowercase 'deepseek1' and uppercase 'DEEPSEEK1' searches returned exactly one matching title card.
Tone & Format expanded to show helper copy and Tone/Format comboboxes, then collapsed with details hidden and summary 'Tone: Concise · Format: Numbered Steps' still visible.
Tone combobox contained exactly: None, Professional, Friendly, Concise, Technical.
Format combobox contained exactly: None, Paragraphs, Bulleted List, Numbered Steps, Markdown Table.
After choosing Professional and Bulleted List and collapsing the section, the visible summary read 'Tone: Professional · Format: Bulleted List'.
With Concise and Numbered Steps selected, using 'deepseek1' injected its body plus the visible concise-tone instruction and numbered-steps format instruction into Claude's prompt textbox.
Grouped library was visible with named Group1 and YouTube headers plus Ungrouped; cards showed titles, body previews, Use buttons, usage counts, and a variable badge where applicable.
The library contains existing non-AutoTest user prompts. Reaching the no-prompts state would require destructive removal of user data, and no empty-library fixture is provided.
After pinning 'deepseek1', its menu correctly changed to Unpin, but no PINNED section appeared above the grouped library; the card remained under Group1.

Using the Group1 prompt with body 'test updated2' moved its card ahead of 'deepseek1' in the next snapshot. The current UI applies recency within grouped sections and does not display a RECENT header.
Using AutoTest-TC054 inserted its full body into Claude's prompt textbox (with the active tone/format modifiers), and the PromptMate panel remained open.
AutoTest-TC054 usage count changed from 1× to 2× immediately after clicking Use.
Pinning 'deepseek1' changed its action state to pinned, but no PINNED section appeared and the prompt remained under Group1 instead of moving to a separate pinned section.
On https://chatgpt.com/, the authenticated ChatGPT page loaded and a visible accessible button named 'Open PromptMate' was present.
Using AutoTest-TC054 inserted its body and active modifiers into ChatGPT's contenteditable textbox while the panel stayed open. The pre-existing unsent draft 'why' was restored after verification.

Unpinning 'deepseek1' removed the visible Pinned section after sync settled and restored the prompt under Group1 with the group count increasing from 2 to 3. Current UI uses grouped sections rather than a RECENT header.

Pinned section ultimately showed both deepseek1 and AutoTest-TC054 simultaneously, while Group1 count dropped to 1. The second card took roughly four seconds to move after its menu already reported Unpin.
New prompt opened a dialog with Title placeholder 'E.g. Improve tone', Group selector, Prompt body placeholder 'Write your prompt here…', Cancel, and Save.
Created AutoTest-TC032 with body 'This is an automated test prompt.'; after Saving completed, the dialog closed and the title/body were visible in the library.
Entered AutoTest-TC033-cancel and a body, clicked Cancel, and confirmed the dialog closed with no matching prompt card created.
With title empty and body populated, Save kept the New prompt dialog open and surfaced status 'Title and prompt body are required.'

With title AutoTest-TC035 and a confirmed empty body, Save kept the dialog open and surfaced 'Title and prompt body are required.' An AutoTest-empty-body fixture was created during setup and retained for the later Trash tests.
Edit on AutoTest-TC032 opened an 'Edit prompt' dialog pre-filled with title AutoTest-TC032 and body 'This is an automated test prompt.'
Changed the created prompt to title AutoTest-TC037-edited and body 'This is the edited automated test prompt.'; after save completed, exactly one card showed the new body and the title was present.
Changed the edit title to AutoTest-TC038-discard, clicked Cancel, and confirmed that title was absent while the prior edited body remained visible.
Cleared the edit title with select-all/backspace, clicked Save, and confirmed the Edit prompt dialog remained open with 'Title and prompt body are required.'
Restored a non-empty title, cleared the prompt body, clicked Save, and confirmed the dialog remained open with 'Title and prompt body are required.'

Clicking Copy on the prompt with body 'This is the edited automated test prompt.' left the browser clipboard empty; a paste attempt reported no clipboard data. The pre-existing ChatGPT draft was restored afterward.
Version history opened for AutoTest-TC037-edited, showed a Current revision, two prior revisions with Show changes and Restore actions, and the 10-revision/restoring guidance copy.
Show changes on the earlier revision changed to Hide changes and revealed Title and Body diffs with explicit insertion/deletion markup, including the edited body words.
Clicking Hide changes removed the diff markup; no Hide changes button remained and both prior revisions again showed Show changes.

Restore opened a JavaScript confirmation, but accepting it caused the Chrome controller to hang and reset repeatedly. A fresh ChatGPT tab still showed the edited body, so successful restore/new-revision creation could not be confirmed.

No prompt with more than 10 revisions was available. The most revision-rich visible candidate, 'test promtp1' with body 'test updated2', showed 8 entries, so the 10-entry cap precondition was not met.
Deleting the run-created AutoTest-empty-body prompt removed its card from the main library and reduced the Ungrouped count from 6 to 5 after sync settled.
PromptMate menu's 'Recently deleted' opened the Trash view. AutoTest-empty-body was listed first with 'Deleted today · expires in 30 days' plus Restore and Delete forever actions.
On https://chat.deepseek.com/, the DeepSeek chat page loaded and a visible accessible button named 'Open PromptMate' was present.
Using AutoTest-TC054 inserted its body and active modifiers into DeepSeek's Message DeepSeek textbox with paragraph breaks, while the PromptMate panel remained open.
Restore removed AutoTest-empty-body from Trash after sync settled. Returning via Prompt library showed the prompt visible under Ungrouped and restored the group count to 6.
Skipped per runbook: Delete forever is irreversible and the user did not separately approve permanent deletion scope.
PromptMate menu switched to Recently deleted/Trash, then Prompt library restored the grouped library with AutoTest-empty-body visible.
Using AutoTest-empty-body triggered a visible 'Syncing…' indicator, which then became hidden within the wait window.
After a full DeepSeek page reload and reopening PromptMate, AutoTest-empty-body, the edited AutoTest prompt body, and AutoTest-TC054 were all still present.
User Guide opened a new Chrome tab titled 'PromptMate Guide — Save, organize, and use prompts in one click' at jitangupta.com/promptmate/getting-started/.
Rate PromptMate opened a new Chrome tab at the PromptMate Chrome Web Store reviews URL.
Request a feature opened a new tab titled 'PromptMate — Feature Request' at the expected Google Forms URL.
Created a 144-character AutoTest-TC060 title. The title element stayed within the 337.6px card bounds and was single-line clipped/truncated rather than overflowing the card.
Created AutoTest-TC061-20260621 with a 1,109-character body. Use injected all 1,109 characters into DeepSeek; total text was 1,433 characters after active tone/format modifiers.
Saved and injected quotes, rocket emoji, a newline, and literal <script>alert(1)</script> without corruption. The injected text began with the exact saved body before modifiers.
Created two separate prompts titled AutoTest-TC063. Two matching cards were visible with distinct body previews Body A and Body B.
With a New prompt dialog open and unsaved fields populated, pressing Escape left the dialog open; the typed AutoTest-TC064-escape title remained in the modal.
Entering 'Write about {{topic}} for {{audience}}' showed 'DETECTED VARIABLES · 2', listed both placeholders, and retained the double-brace helper text.
Saved AutoTest-TC066 with two placeholders; its card displayed a '2 variables' badge beside the Use action.
Use opened 'Fill in variables' for AutoTest-TC066 with two labeled inputs, detected-variable count, live preview, Cancel, and a disabled Insert button.
Insert was disabled initially and after only TOPIC was filled, then became enabled once both TOPIC and AUDIENCE had non-empty values.
Live preview resolved both placeholders and displayed 'Write about AI testing for product teams'.
Preview on AutoTest-TC066 opened the variable fill-in flow with TOPIC and AUDIENCE fields, live preview, and Insert disabled until values are supplied.
After the required 3-second wait, https://kimi.com redirected to www.kimi.com and displayed the accessible 'Open PromptMate' button. Kimi itself was anonymous, but its chat textbox was usable.
Using AutoTest-empty-body inserted its body and active modifiers into Kimi's chat textbox with paragraph breaks, while the PromptMate panel remained open.
Filled TOPIC='AI testing' and AUDIENCE='product teams', clicked Insert, and Kimi received 'Write about AI testing for product teams' with no unresolved {{tokens}}; the dialog closed.
Library showed named Group1 (1), YouTube (1), and Ungrouped (12) headers with accurate visible counts.
Clicking Group1 hid its test updated2 prompt card; clicking the same header again restored the card.
Group1 actions menu exposed Rename, Edit instruction, and Delete group controls.
Created AutoTest-Group with AutoTest-TC074-fixture, renamed it inline to AutoTest-Renamed-Group, and confirmed the prompt remained beneath the renamed header.
Edit instruction opened the Group instruction dialog for AutoTest-Renamed-Group with Use instruction switch, optional instruction field, ordering copy, Cancel, and Save.
Enabled Use instruction, saved 'Always answer in first person.', reopened the dialog, and confirmed aria-checked=true with the exact saved text present.
Grouped prompt insertion produced: group instruction, then prompt body, then active tone/format modifiers. User context was not enabled/present; the instruction preceded the body as promised.
Moved AutoTest-empty-body from Ungrouped into AutoTest-Renamed-Group. After sync settled, the target group count increased to 2 and the prompt appeared beneath that header.
Your context opened with Use context switch, ABOUT YOU textbox, Pull it from this chat, on-device privacy copy, Cancel, and Add context.
Saved Use context off and confirmed aria-checked=false after reopening; then saved it on and confirmed aria-checked=true on the next reopen.
Saved ABOUT YOU as 'AutoTest user context for PromptMate QA.' and confirmed the exact text persisted when Your context was reopened.
Kimi displayed a login modal when the synthetic context seed was submitted. Not logged in - user handles auth manually; the modal was dismissed without attempting authentication.
With Use context enabled, insertion produced the saved AutoTest context first, then the prompt body, then tone/format modifiers.
After disabling Use context, the same prompt body and modifiers were inserted but the saved AutoTest context text was absent.
Preview opened a dialog showing the resolved prompt content while Kimi's main chat textbox remained empty.

The pm_test_key/pm_test_value fixture URL retained its parameters and repeatedly reloaded Kimi: the Open PromptMate button alternated between present and absent, preventing the panel from staying open. The A QUICK FAVOR banner never became visible.
TC-087 did not produce a stable visible rating banner because the fixture URL entered a reload loop, so dismiss-and-reopen behavior could not be executed.
Created AutoTest-TC054-20260621 on claude.ai, then navigated to chatgpt.com; the exact title and body appeared after PromptMate loaded and sync settled.
